headlines.text: Iran FM Araghchi Heads to Pakistan as US-Iran Talks Deadlocked on Day 56
Trump Extends Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire by Three Weeks, Says 'Don't Rush Me' on Iran
Hegseth: US Naval Blockade of Iran 'Growing and Going Global,' Another Carrier Incoming
DOJ Drops Criminal Probe Into Fed Chair Powell; Trump's Pick Kevin Warsh Clears Path
Meta Announces 8,000 Layoffs — 10% of Workforce — to Fund AI Spending Binge
Intel Surges 19%+ After Earnings Beat, Elon Musk Hints at $3B Deal With Chipmaker
Senate GOP Advances $70 Billion Funding Plan for ICE and Border Patrol
DOJ Seeks to Denaturalize Hundreds of US Citizens Amid Immigration Crackdown
Michael Jackson Biopic Opens Today, Eyes Record-Breaking $150M Global Debut
Gas Prices Tick Back Up to $4.06 Nationally as Iran War Keeps Oil Markets on Edge

stories.hed: Araghchi Flies to Islamabad as US-Iran Talks Hit a Wall
stories.dek: Iran's top diplomat is heading to Pakistan for 'consultations' — not actually talking to Americans, just talking about talking to Americans. Trump, asked for a timeline on ending the war he started, offered the diplomatic masterpiece: 'Don't rush me.'

stories.hed: Russia Offers to Store Iran's Enriched Uranium — Trump Says No Thanks
stories.dek: Putin proposed stashing Iran's uranium in Russia, which could have theoretically unlocked a deal. Trump reportedly declined, apparently not wild about giving Moscow more nuclear leverage. At least someone is being cautious about something.

stories.hed: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Clearing Way for Trump's Fed Pick Kevin Warsh
stories.dek: The Justice Department closed its criminal investigation into outgoing Fed Chair Jerome Powell, who had been scrutinized over a building renovation. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq moved higher on the news, because markets love it when the president gets what he wants.

stories.hed: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs While Doubling Down on AI Spending to $135B
stories.dek: Zuckerberg is firing 10% of his staff to pay for AI infrastructure — a strategy that, in 2026, is called 'efficiency.' Microsoft simultaneously offered voluntary buyouts to thousands of senior employees, because apparently Big Tech is having a moment.

stories.hed: Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low — Below COVID, Below 2008, Below Everything
stories.dek: The University of Michigan's final April consumer sentiment reading came in at 49.8, the lowest ever recorded — worse than the financial crisis, worse than the pandemic. On the bright side, at least people feel consistently terrible, which is its own kind of stability.

markets.context: Nasdaq and S&P rallied after the DOJ dropped its Powell probe and Intel surged 19% on a strong earnings beat, while the Dow lagged as oil dipped on ceasefire extension hopes.

gas.note: Still $1+ above pre-war prices — turns out blockading the world's busiest oil chokepoint has consequences at the pump.

popCulture.hed: Michael Jackson Biopic Opens Today With Record $150M Global Box Office Projection — Critics Hate It, Fans Don't Care
popCulture.dek: Lionsgate's 'Michael,' directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring MJ's nephew Jaafar Jackson, lands in 3,900 theaters today with a 39% critics score and a 94% audience score on Rotten Tomatoes. It's tracking to be the biggest music biopic opening ever, proving once again that critical consensus and box office returns have a complicated relationship.

genZ.definition: An AI-generated brainrot character that exploded across TikTok this month, featuring an absurd looping figure dancing to chaotic audio — the latest in a lineage of unhinged AI meme creatures that Gen Z has fully adopted as their spirit animals. It pairs with the viral 'I just get so nervy' audio from a Roblox voice chat clip, creating the perfectly meaningless loop that defines 2026 internet culture.
genZ.usage: When Trump said 'Don't rush me' about ending the Iran war, the entire TikTok comment section responded with Tung Tung Tung Sahur dancing, which is honestly a more coherent foreign policy response than anything out of the State Department.

tldr: Day 56 of the Iran war: Iran's top diplomat is flying to Pakistan to talk about maybe talking to the US, Trump extended the Lebanon ceasefire and told reporters not to rush him, and Intel's earnings beat briefly made people forget that consumer sentiment just hit an all-time record low. It's fine.
